title: Xylophragma harleyi
intro: Xylophragma harleyi is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly known as the "Harley's Xylophragma" or "Xylophragma". The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of infections and inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Xylophragma harleyi include iridoid glycosides, which have been isolated and studied for their potential medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Xylophragma harleyi belongs to the family Bignoniaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the Bignonia family. This family comprises over 140 genera and 1,000 species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. Xylophragma harleyi is a species of flowering plant that is endemic to Brazil. The classification of Xylophragma harleyi also includes its placement within the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The classification of Xylophragma harleyi is a reflection of its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
